title: Ferroelectric hysteresis in singly aligned graphene-hBN moiré superlattices
authors: Bao Q. Tu, Tanweer Ahmed, Garen Avedissian, Suzanne Lancaster, Mayank Sharma, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Fèlix Casanova, Marco Gobbi, Luis E. Hueso
abstract: Ferroelectric materials have the unique ability to maintain an electric polarization which can be reversed under an external applied electric field. This property makes them valuable for applications such as non-volatile random-access memories, transducers, actuators and electro optic modulators. Recently, emergent unconventional ferroelectricity has been demonstrated in moiré superlattices of bilayer graphene and hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) hosting non centrosymmetric stacking order. Whether this phenomenon is also present in noncentrosymmetric single layer graphene (SLG)-hBN moiré superlattices is still under debate. Here we demonstrate a ferroelectric response in an SLG-hBN moiré superlattice. Through Hall measurements, we pinpoint the origin of the hysteretic behavior to abnormal charge screening due to the moiré superlattice band and estimate the spontaneous polarization magnitude in the moiré superlattice structure. Temperature dependent measurements confirm that the hysteretic behavior persists from 2K up to room temperature, opening opportunities for high-mobility, ultrathin non-volatile devices
summary: The researchers successfully demonstrated ferroelectric hysteresis in single-layer graphene/hBN moiré superlattices, proving that single-interface alignment is sufficient to induce this effect. By analyzing the $n_{ext} - E_{ext}$ phase space, they attributed the hysteresis to abnormal charge screening within the moiré band. This work establishes a promising, scalable platform for developing ultrathin, non-volatile electronic devices.
main_problem: To determine if ferroelectric hysteresis exists in single-layer graphene (SLG)-hBN moiré superlattices, contrasting with previous work on doubly-aligned systems.
main_result: A robust ferroelectric response was demonstrated in the SLG-hBN moiré superlattice, persisting from 2K to room temperature, suggesting a viable platform for non-volatile electronics.
method: Electrical characterization using Hall measurements and sweeping the external electric field ($E_{ext}$) and carrier concentration ($n_{ext}$) to observe hysteretic behavior.
model_or_system: The physical system is the SLG-hBN moiré superlattice, where the moiré potential dictates the electronic structure and charge screening mechanisms.
key_observables: Hysteretic behavior in sheet resistance ($R_S$) vs. $E_{ext}$, deviation of the Charge Neutrality Point (CNP) in the $n_{ext} - E_{ext}$ phase space, and the spontaneous polarization magnitude.
important_parameters: Temperature range (2K to 300K), external electric field ($E_{ext}$), and carrier concentration ($n_{ext}$).
main_assumptions: The hysteretic behavior is attributed to abnormal charge screening due to the moiré superlattice band structure, and that single alignment is sufficient to induce the effect.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate device schematics, $R_S$ vs. $n_{ext}$ and $n_{ext} - E_{ext}$ phase space diagrams showing hysteresis loops, and temperature dependence confirming the effect's stability.
paper_structure: The paper systematically investigates the ferroelectric response by comparing different alignment conditions, mapping the $n_{ext} - E_{ext}$ phase space, quantifying the hysteresis, and confirming the temperature stability of the effect.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter electronics, the study of emergent polarization and charge screening in moiré heterostructures touches upon concepts of broken symmetry and emergent order parameters relevant to quantum materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
